title: Node 24, TypeScript 7.0.2, TypeScript 6 API Compatibility, npm, and Reproducible Installs · CI/CD Control Room
summary: A delivery pairing session predicts each automation transition before producing the first working reproducible TypeScript build contract for node 24, typescript 7.0.2, typescript 6 api compatibility, npm, and reproducible installs. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of reproducible TypeScript build contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Align Node 24, npm 11 or later, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API required by current tooling, package metadata, engines, action runtime, and local developer versions.
Use npm ci with a committed matching lockfile and committed install flags while detecting lock drift and registry changes.
Bound dependency lifecycle scripts, registry authentication, private package access, network egress, install auditing, and secret exposure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Node Npm Typescript Version Contract
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript build floats Node and npm, mutates its lockfile, runs dependency lifecycle scripts, calls a programmatic API that TypeScript 7 does not provide, and passes only with warm local residue. Prove Node 24, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API, npm ci, clean output, and changed-environment behavior. Competency probe: Align Node 24, npm 11 or later, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API required by current tooling, package metadata, engines, action runtime, and local developer versions.

steps.instruction:

Tailor its typescript-build.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. content.code:
# Evidence: ci-reproducibility-changed-environment-1carxa
  - competency: ci-reproducibility-changed-environment
    artifact: typescript-build.yaml
    decision: reproducibility changed environment
    environment: reusable workflow called by three repositories at different revisions
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: runner image runtime version authority network and teardown record
    case-key: 1carxa
    change: |
      node: 24-lts
      npm: 11-or-later
      typescript-native: 7.0.2
      typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
      tsc: native-compiler
      api-tools: typescript6-alias
      setup-node: 7
      install: npm-ci-frozen-lock
      package-manager-cache: risk-reviewed
      workspace: clean-checkout
      build-output: content-digested
      changed-environment: cold-cache-and-locale
    verify: npm ci then run strict type test build and package checks from a clean supported Node 24 environment
    failure: Reject floating toolchains, npm install lock mutation, local residue, lifecycle-script trust, or one warm build as reproducibility.
    repair: Align tool versions, freeze installs, bound registry and scripts, build cleanly, digest output, and repeat changed environments.

workshop case — A workshop TypeScript build floats Node and npm, mutates its lockfile, runs dependency lifecycle scripts, calls a programmatic API that TypeScript 7 does not provide, and passes only with warm local residue. options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, copy one familiar workflow, omit event and revision identity, permissions, runner, immutable artifact, failure, rollback, and disposable delivery-environment transfer checks, then accept a green run as production proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Ci Reproducibility Changed Environment condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
